_methodology: Observed live state via SSH on 2026-05-04. Hierarchy applied per manual: code → running processes → DB state → URLs → filesystem. Internal RFC1918 LAN IPs are placeheld as <vm-ip> / <lan-host-ip> to match the public repo's scrub policy; physical addressing is not load-bearing for the architectural model. Real values exist on the operating host.

hosts.name: msgschool
hosts.purpose: single-tenant production host for the msgschool Telegram bot, the toolsd daemon, postgres, and the Cloudflare tunnel that exposes the webhook.

hosts.name: the KVM host
hosts.purpose: physical KVM host that runs the msgschool VM. Also the rsync target for nightly age-encrypted backups (forced-command SSH only). Hosts ~7 other VMs unrelated to msgschool.

components.name: msgschool — Telegram webhook + landing pages
components.description: Next.js 16 app (App Router) that serves the public site (/, /privacy, /terms, /security) and the Telegram webhook (POST /api/bot/webhook). The webhook is the single entry point for user-facing traffic: it validates the Telegram secret-token header, runs the inbound credential scrubber, dispatches active sessions to OpenClaw, and runs the outbound credential scrubber on every reply before sendMessage. If down: Telegram retries the webhook for up to 24 hours; messages received during the outage land late but not lost; the public site returns 502.
components.restartCount: unknown — systemd does not surface this without parsing journal
components.purpose: the single user-facing surface of the product; everything else is plumbing
components.expectedLoad: ≤4 questions/day per user × current 4 users ≈ 16 webhook calls/day. Modeled to 600 users (~2400/day) for capacity planning.
components.provenLoad: 244 ms_messages rows total since 2026-04-19 — average ~16 messages/day across 4 users. Source: SELECT count(*) FROM ms_messages.

components.name: msgschool-toolsd — Canvas + Skyward tool daemon
components.description: Long-running Node service that holds decrypted Canvas/Skyward credentials in process memory only and exposes a unix-socket JSON-RPC surface (canvas.list_courses, canvas.get_pulse, skyward.get_grades, skyward.get_attendance, pulse.combined, tools.healthcheck, ...). The agent calls in via /usr/local/bin/ms_call. Holds plaintext creds for ≤10 minutes per user (LRU cache). On startup, hydrates every user's tmpfs creds from on-disk ciphertext via ensureAllUsersHydrated(); without this, a reboot leaves users with empty tmpfs and silently broken Canvas/Skyward fetches (incident 2026-05-01). If down: every Canvas/Skyward read fails with INTERNAL/transport-error; bot replies still work; agent has no usable data.
components.purpose: the structural-ish boundary that keeps credential plaintext out of the LLM agent's process. v1 caveat: agent and toolsd run as the same Linux UID (root), so the isolation is behavioral (playbook rules + outbound scrubber + credscan) rather than structural per-UID. Per-UID work blocked on OpenClaw setuid support.
components.expectedLoad: ≤8 tool calls per user per question; ~30/min steady at scale
components.provenLoad: 17 ms_tool_calls rows since wired 2026-05-01. Source: SELECT count(*) FROM ms_tool_calls.

components.name: openclaw-gateway — agent runtime
components.description: OpenClaw gateway that runs each user's LLM agent. msgschool dispatches to it via a local TCP loopback (127.0.0.1:18789 / :18791). Loads the per-user workspace at /opt/msgschool/users/canvasagent-<tg_id>/workspace and runs the agent against the configured LLM provider chain (see `llmModels.rotation` below for the current primary + fallback). If down: every active-state Telegram message hits the bot but produces no agent reply; bot logs `<dispatch failed>`.
env: unknown (managed by openclaw, not by msgschool .env)
components._versionUpgradeNote: Upgraded 2026-05-04 from 2026.4.15 (041266a) to 2026.5.3-1 after release-notes review for session-jsonl/usage-block compatibility, [SYSTEM] prompt convention, gateway socket layout, and openclaw.json schema. No regressions observed; live canvas.connectivity_probe ok in 452ms post-restart.
components.purpose: third-party agent runtime; we don't own its source. msgschool treats it as a black box that consumes [SYSTEM] prompts and emits replies.
components.provenLoad: matches msgschool — every active inbound triggers exactly one openclaw turn

components.name: msgschool-credscan — periodic credential leak sweeper
components.description: Systemd-timer-driven sweep that runs every 2 minutes. For each agent workspace, reads the user's currently-loaded credential strings from /run/msgschool/<agent>/<svc>.json, greps every text-shaped file in the workspace for those literal bytes, replaces matches in place with [redacted-credscan]. Also scans OpenClaw session jsonls and warns to journalctl if any credential-shaped lines appear (those can't be live-edited). If down: workspaces accumulate any credential bytes the inbound/outbound scrubbers happened to miss; outbound scrubber + behavioral playbook rules still hold.
components.purpose: defense-in-depth net under the inbound + outbound scrubbers. Catches credential bytes that landed in workspace files (USER.md, MEMORY.md, agent-written scripts) before they end up in a backup tarball.
components.expectedLoad: fires 720× per day; each run does up to a few hundred grep ops
components.provenLoad: unknown — timer logs to journalctl with tag=credscan

components.name: msgschool-purge-archived — daily 30-day purge
components.description: Systemd-timer-driven daily purge of workspace archives older than 30 days. Backs the privacy promise that deletion-archived workspaces are auto-purged after 30 days. If down: the .archive_pre_toolsd_* (and similar) directories grow indefinitely; eventually bites disk.
components.purpose: the cron half of the user-facing /delete privacy promise

components.name: postgresql 16 — application database
components.description: Local PostgreSQL 16 instance bound to 127.0.0.1 only. Holds the bot's user/state/message/audit tables (ms_*) plus legacy OCMarketplace tables that share the database. If down: webhook handler 500s on every inbound; agent dispatch fails; recoveries via the daily age-encrypted dump.
components.purpose: single source of truth for user state, message log, audit trails
components.expectedLoad: small — ~hundreds of writes per day at current scale, modeled to ~10k/day at 600 users
components.provenLoad: 9015 kB total db size, 244 ms_messages rows

components.name: cloudflared — Cloudflare tunnel
components.description: Outbound-only Cloudflare tunnel that connects the msgschool VM to Cloudflare's edge so that msgschool.com (and the Telegram webhook) can reach the Next.js process on 127.0.0.1:3010 without opening any inbound port. Runs under a dedicated 'cloudflared' system user (uid 999) since 2026-05-04. If down: msgschool.com returns 5xx via Cloudflare; the Telegram webhook fails; messages queue at Telegram for up to 24h.
components.bindAddress: 127.0.0.1 (local metrics endpoint; outbound to Cloudflare edge)
components.runtimeVersion: unknown — installed via Cloudflare's apt repo
components.expectedLoad: matches msgschool; tunnel pushes Telegram traffic

connections.description: Telegram POSTs to https://msgschool.com/api/bot/webhook with x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token header. Cloudflare edge → tunnel → 127.0.0.1:3010.
Active-state messages dispatched via the gateway's local HTTP endpoint :18789

Primary + fallback both route to Moonshot AI's Kimi platform (K2.6 primary, K2.5 fallback as of 2026-05-13). When the routing changes, regenerate this block and the llmModels.rotation section together.
ms_call wrapper invoked from the agent's bash tool; talks to /run/msgschool/toolsd.sock
REST fetches against <district>.instructure.com with Bearer token from credentials/canvas.json (in-memory only)
Headless Chromium login + DOM scrape against student.<district>.org
Audit row INSERT into ms_tool_calls per request
Daily age-encrypted backups (db dump + workspaces + config). the KVM host side accepts only the rsync forced-command — verified 2026-05-01.

routes.title: MsgSchool
routes.description: Public landing page. Hero + privacy stripe + How-it-works + capabilities grid + CTA.

routes.title: Security — MsgSchool
routes.description: Public security architecture page. Documents the four defense layers, what we cannot defend against, and what to do on a credential leak. Linked from header, footer, and the home-page privacy stripe.

routes.title: Privacy — MsgSchool
routes.description: Public privacy policy. Legal-style document; defers to /security for full architecture.

Telegram webhook ingress. Validates x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token header against TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET env. Runs inbound credential scrubber on every message body BEFORE logging or dispatching. The single non-public-page endpoint of the application.
routes.hits7d: 244 since 2026-04-19 (count of ms_messages rows of any direction)

siteMap._notes: No /admin route exists yet. ADMIN_PANEL_SPEC.md is a draft spec; no code has been written against it. /admin would be operator-internal and password-gated.

datastores.name: msgschool postgres
datastores._sharedInstanceNote: The 'msgschool' database co-resides with legacy OCMarketplace tables (accounts, agents, sessions, users, ...) on the same cluster. msgschool's tables are prefixed ms_* to avoid collision. When OCMarketplace is fully retired, a single rename migration will drop the prefix.
datastores.backupPolicy: daily age-encrypted pg_dump → /var/backups/msgschool → rsync to <kvm-host>; 14-day local retention; offline private decryption key

schemas.name: public
schemas.purpose: default; holds both ms_* (msgschool) and legacy OCMarketplace tables

tables.name: ms_users
tables.purpose: one row per Telegram account that has ever messaged the bot. Identity is the numeric Telegram user_id (stable, unforgeable). State machine column drives the bot's reply.
tables._piiNote: Per /privacy posture, telegram_user_id is treated as not-PII (opaque integer not derivable from a phone/email/name and not searchable on Telegram's surface). telegram_username and telegram_first_name are mutable display fields not used for auth and never rendered in operator analytics.

tables.name: ms_messages
tables.purpose: inbound + outbound platform audit trail. Used for debug, abuse review, and the rate limiter. Not used for conversation context — the agent keeps its own memory in the workspace.
tables._piiNote: On detected credential pastes, the row's text column stores `[scrubbed: <field>]` not the raw value. Outbound rows store opaque markers like `<agent reply>`; the literal reply text is not persisted.

tables.name: ms_tool_calls
tables.purpose: audit log for every msgschool-toolsd RPC. Records metadata only (no credential value, no response body).

tables.name: ms_drizzle_migrations
tables.purpose: drizzle-kit migration history specific to msgschool (avoids collision with the legacy 'drizzle_migrations' table)

roles.name: msgschool
grants: owner of all ms_* tables and sequences (ms_users, ms_messages, ms_code_redemptions, ms_tool_calls, ms_drizzle_migrations)

datastores._migrationsPendingSource: git ls drizzle/*.sql vs SELECT count(*) FROM ms_drizzle_migrations: 2 == 2

topLevelDirs.purpose: npm install output (Next.js 16 + drizzle + grammy + pg + playwright-core)
application source — bot handler, toolsd, schema
design + ops + spec docs (architecture, security, toolsd spec, admin panel spec, ...)
provision/deprovision/install scripts + the credscan timer body
ms_call client wrapper (installed to /usr/local/bin/ms_call by install-toolsd.sh)

dataRoots.purpose: per-user agent workspaces — one canvasagent-<tg_id>/ subtree per Telegram user. Mode 0700 on the workspace root.
daily age-encrypted backups (db dump + workspaces tarball + config tarball)

dataRoots.sizeMb: unknown — included in postgres engine accounting; total db is 9 MB

dataRoots.purpose: tmpfs-backed plaintext credential cache + the toolsd unix socket. Wiped on reboot; rehydrated by ensureAllUsersHydrated() on toolsd start.
platform .env (Telegram token, DATABASE_URL, webhook secret). Mode 0640 root:readystack — flagged in security audit M4 to be tightened to 0600 root:root.
OpenClaw agent config + sessions + bundled chromium user-data

disk.uploadsFileCount: unknown — three workspaces, each with platform-symlinked + agent-writable files
disk.logsTotalMb: unknown — journalctl-managed, capped by systemd config

details._ciNote: No CI pipeline. No GitHub Actions, no Vercel build. Deploy is manual via scripts/install-toolsd.sh on the prod VM. Flagged in security audit as a gap.

tests._summary: 46 unit tests across 15 suites in 2 test files. No integration tests, no e2e, no UI tests.

security._unauthenticatedNote: These four are read-only marketing/legal/security pages; intended public. The webhook endpoint is publicly reachable but rejects requests without the x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token header matching TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET.
security._adminNote: No /admin route exists yet. Spec at docs/ADMIN_PANEL_SPEC.md proposes /admin behind ADMIN_PASSWORD cookie + middleware; not built.
security.tlsVersion: managed by Cloudflare edge — TLS 1.3 default
security.hstsEnabled: unknown — Cloudflare-controlled, not in our nginx
security._cspNote: Not yet set on the Next.js side. Cloudflare may add edge-level headers but not verified.
security._cookiesNote: No app-side cookies. The bot has no logged-in web session; auth is via Telegram identity routed through the webhook secret.

webhookSignatureVerification._source: src/app/api/bot/webhook/route.ts:19-21 reads x-telegram-bot-api-secret-token and compares to TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET

openCves._note: Dev-server only; not exposed in production runtime

security._openCvesSource: npm audit --json on 2026-05-04 (post Next 16.2.4 bump) — total {moderate:6, high:0}. The Next.js high (GHSA-ggv3-7p47-pfv8 HTTP request smuggling) and the next/image cache CVE (GHSA-3x4c-7xq6-9pq8) closed by upgrading next 16.1.6 → 16.2.4 in commit ac92933.

exceptions.what: Agent process and msgschool-toolsd run as the same Linux UID (root). The agent could in principle `cat /run/msgschool/<agent>/canvas.json` and read plaintext.
exceptions.why: OpenClaw does not yet expose a per-agent setuid hook to launch the agent under a non-root UID. The defense today is behavioral (playbook hard-rules forbid reading the credential file or launching playwright + outbound scrubber + credscan timer) plus the structural fact that the agent has no playbook path that requires the credential value.

exceptions.what: Backup tarballs only land on the msgschool VM and on the KVM host. No third destination off-LAN.
exceptions.why: Adds a Spaces/B2/S3 dependency we haven't wired. Tarballs are age-encrypted with the private key held offline, so the leak risk is mitigated; the loss-risk (both VM and the KVM host dying together) is open.

exceptions.what: AppArmor profiles for msgschool + msgschool-toolsd are authored, deployed, and loaded in complain mode at the kernel level, but the runtime processes are not being attached to them. systemd's AppArmorProfile= directive failed with 'no such file or directory' when wired in (rolled back); path-based attach via /usr/bin/npx isn't auto-binding to the systemd-launched npx process either.
exceptions.why: Path-vs-name attach mechanism on Ubuntu 24.04 with systemd needs more iteration — likely switching the profile target to /usr/bin/node with stricter exec stack, or using aa-exec wrapping in ExecStart. Profiles are loaded so the structural surface is ready; runtime attach is the remaining piece. Defense-in-depth value of the file-level deployment is partial: file permissions and the kernel's path-based denials (deny /etc/shadow etc.) won't fire until processes are matched.

exceptions.what: Headless Chromium spawned by openclaw-gateway and by msgschool-toolsd Skyward sessions runs with --no-sandbox.
exceptions.why: Sandbox under non-root needs unprivileged user namespaces and chromium-bundled support; running as root with sandbox is also blocked. Today the only navigated origins are *.instructure.com and student.<district>.org — both trusted.

security.dataResidency: United States — single VM behind Cloudflare US edge
security._piiLocationsNote: Per /privacy posture, telegram_user_id is treated as not-PII. Raw message text is never persisted (scrubbed labels only). No name/email/phone/address is collected. No piiLocations claimed.

encryptionAtRest.method: provider-default (LUKS/dm-crypt at the host VM disk layer is operator's choice; not enforced by app)
systemd-creds (machine-bound key held by the host kernel)
age (recipient pubkey on host; private key held offline)

incidentLog.summary: ensureAllUsersHydrated() defined but never called — 11-day silent breakage of Tona's account post-reboot. Wired into msgschool-toolsd startup; verified all users back online.

Inbound credential scrubber half-fired on 'unclassified' detections: DB row redacted but Telegram message left in chat AND forwarded to agent in plaintext. Fixed by branching on detection.hasAnyCredential rather than fields.length.

Agent observed echoing real credentials to Telegram chat. Triggered design + cutover of msgschool-toolsd + outbound scrubber + credscan timer + playbook hard rules forbidding reading the credential file or writing playwright scripts.

security.threatModel: Single-tenant home-VM Telegram agent with per-user credentials for school portals (Canvas, Skyward). Highest-impact threat is leakage of a parent's Skyward password (account-takeover surface for the family's school account: address, emergency contacts, possibly schedule changes). Defense layers: (1) inbound scrubber deletes credential pastes from chat + redacts in DB; (2) playbook rules forbid the agent from reading credential files, writing scripts that contain credentials, calling Canvas/Skyward directly, or echoing credentials in chat; (3) outbound scrubber strips literal credential bytes from every reply; (4) credscan timer sweeps every workspace every 2 minutes for plaintext credential occurrences. Out of scope: VM-root compromise, Telegram-account takeover, Telegram-side retention of deleted messages. See https://msgschool.com/security for the user-facing version.

observability.metrics: none — no Prometheus / Grafana / Datadog. journalctl is the only telemetry.
observability._alertingNote: No paging. The healthcheck endpoint planned in the admin-panel spec is the closest scheduled replacement.

history._squashNote: Git history was squashed to a single commit on 2026-05-03 immediately before the repo was flipped public. Pre-squash development was 107 commits in private; post-squash there is one commit and no archaeology.
docTodos: ADMIN_PANEL_SPEC.md is a draft; no code written yet
TOOLSD_SPEC.md §1 lists per-UID isolation as deferred — still open
PRIVACY_DEFENSIBILITY_WORKPLAN.md predates toolsd cutover; some items now done (encryption-at-rest), others still open (off-host backups, /delete fully wired)
OPS.md SSH alias `msgschool` referenced but not in this user's ssh config; deploy guide assumes operator added it manually

msgschool.com.ingress: Cloudflare Tunnel (no inbound A/AAAA pointing at the VM)

llmModels._summary: Every agent runs the same primary→fallback rotation. Current routing flipped to Moonshot Kimi K2.6 primary + K2.5 fallback on 2026-05-13 (replacing xAI Grok). Recorded in /root/.openclaw/openclaw.json; user-facing copy on msgschool.com is vendor-agnostic so swaps don't require site updates.

rotation.purpose: primary path for every agent turn; Instant Mode (thinking off) for deterministic Canvas/Skyward playbook execution + low Telegram-reply latency

automatic fallback when K2.6 is degraded or returns 5xx; same provider, so won't survive a Moonshot-wide outage — Anthropic key is configured in models.providers.anthropic if a cross-provider safety net needs to be re-engaged

llmModels._appliedToNote: Placeholder Telegram IDs match the repo-content scrub policy. Real IDs exist in /root/.openclaw/openclaw.json on the host.
llmModels.switchTool: direct jq edit + SIGUSR1 to gateway. The legacy scripts/switch-model.sh fleet flip is not in this repo's history; per-swap jq surgery is the current pattern.
llmModels.thinkingDefaultRationale: Forced 'off' for every agent on every model. Rationale was originally observed in Sonnet (2026-04-29 regression — thinking=high made the agent MORE speculative on this narrow task, overrode HARD RULES, offered fake features). Re-validated as the right default for Kimi K2.6 — Instant Mode matches our deterministic playbook better than Thinking Mode (which doubles latency for no upside on structured pulls). See docs/LLM_BEHAVIOR_MITIGATIONS.md §6.
llmModels.providerKeysLocation: /root/.openclaw/openclaw.json (mode 0600 root:root) under models.providers.<provider>.apiKey. Today the active key is models.providers.moonshot.apiKey; dormant blocks for anthropic / xai / openai / openrouter keys remain in the file for fast re-engagement.

externalDeps.purpose: the entire user-facing surface; bot pairs via @MsgSchoolBot
primary + fallback for every agent turn (Kimi K2.6 → K2.5). When the active provider changes, regenerate this entry along with components.openclaw-gateway.description and llmModels.rotation.
REST API for assignments/grades/courses; per-user PAT
externalDeps.credentials: per-user, encrypted at rest in canvas.json.enc

externalDeps.purpose: Family Access portal for grades/attendance/discipline; scraped via headless Chromium
externalDeps.credentials: per-user username+password, encrypted at rest in skyward.json.enc
